title: No replay attack protection in the authentication protocol
description: The password authentication during key exchange computed clientPassword1 = SHA256(password || serverPublicKey). While the server ECDH keypair changes per connection, there was no client-contributed entropy: a captured exchange could theoretically be replayed if the ECDH PRNG was compromised.
recommendation: Add a client-generated per-session random nonce to the ECDSA proof so that even with a compromised ECDH PRNG, each authentication exchange produces a unique, non-replayable value.
patch_description: P7 v1.1 (commit ccfed72) adds a 32-byte SystemRandomNumberGenerator nonce (password_salt field id=17) generated by the client each connection. The ECDSA proof is now derived from SHA256(session_salt || SHA256(plain)) instead of SHA256(plain) alone. Replay is impossible: the session_salt changes every connection and is bound to the specific ECDH keypair of that session. P7 v1.2 (commit f806993) further binds the proof to a server-side per-user stored_salt.
